header.title: SentinelAPI
header.subtitle: Cloud-native intelligent API gateway with JWT auth, adaptive rate limiting, and anomaly-driven auto-blocking

summary.plain_english: SentinelAPI sits in front of an existing backend and adds authentication, abuse protection, and traffic intelligence without requiring backend code changes. It is designed for teams that want a production-style API edge with strong defaults and clear operational controls.

motivation.quote: I wanted to build the kind of gateway layer teams usually adopt only after abuse incidents, and package it so developers can deploy it quickly either from Python or InfraKit.

diagram.alt: SentinelAPI architecture with ALB, ECS gateway, Redis rate limiting, DynamoDB telemetry, and scheduled anomaly detection with auto-blocking

architecture.flow_summary: Client request → ALB → ECS Sentinel gateway (JWT + rate limit + proxy) → upstream API, with telemetry in DynamoDB and anomaly detection Lambda auto-blocking suspicious users.

items: Protects existing APIs without backend rewrites — FastAPI reverse-proxy gateway on ECS Fargate — lets teams add edge security while keeping application code unchanged.
Enforces authenticated access and identity-aware throttling — JWT validation plus Redis token-bucket controls — blocks unauthenticated calls and limits abusive bursts per user.
Detects suspicious traffic patterns automatically — scheduled Lambda anomaly detector on DynamoDB aggregates — flags spikes and can auto-block offending user IDs.
Supports two developer adoption paths — Python deploy library and InfraKit `sentinelapi` resource — fits both app code workflows and IaC-first workflows.
Publishes architecture and usage assets automatically — GitHub Actions + OIDC + S3/CloudFront asset pipeline — keeps portfolio documentation synced with the latest code.
Container startup failed on ECS with `exec format error`; root cause was architecture mismatch, fixed by publishing multi-arch gateway images and pinning deploy image selection.
Lambda Function URL behind Sentinel returned 403 during proxy smoke tests; resolved by ensuring Function URL invoke permissions were correctly provisioned in InfraKit.
CI workflows could leak temporary stacks during cancellations; solved with deterministic run-scoped names and cleanup workflows that tear down by run ID.
Automated diagram rendering failed in GitHub Actions due to Puppeteer sandbox restrictions; fixed by adding a no-sandbox Puppeteer config for Mermaid CLI.
What I'd do differently: add a managed, first-class observability dashboard package (alarms + Grafana board templates) as part of the default deployment output.
